*HN Digest — 2026-07-10*

_Frontier-model day (Grok 4.5, GPT-Live) but the sharper stories are two runtime rewrites (Bun→Rust w/ Kelley's rebuttal; TS 7 in Go) and a split policy day: EU Chat Control 1.0 in, FTC wins Deere right-to-repair._

1. **[AI & agents]** [Grok 4.5](https://x.ai/news/grok-4-5) — 761pts · 1,418c
Why it matters: xAI ships a frontier model + Grok Build CLI, pitched cheaper/faster than Claude Code or Codex.
HN take: "Elon has shown he'll weaponize his platforms against people/companies he doesn't like. Using Grok is a supply-chain risk, and it's not nearly good enough to offset that risk." — _minimaxir_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48835111)

2. **[AI & agents]** [GPT‑Live](https://openai.com/index/introducing-gpt-live/) — 742pts · 517c
Why it matters: OpenAI's realtime voice-to-voice model, pitched as "solved" real-time translation.
HN take: "They most definitely did not solve real-time translation. The French in the video is barely understandable — quality of an American who hasn't used French since high school." — _progval_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48834405)

3. **[Infra & devtools]** [Andrew Kelley on the Bun Rust rewrite](https://andrewkelley.me/post/my-thoughts-bun-rust-rewrite.html) — 670pts · 586c
Why it matters: Zig author responds to Bun (post-Anthropic acquisition) rewriting from Zig to Rust — memory-safety framing plus AI-authored-PR tensions.
HN take: "'Handled by Zig's style guide' ends up as 'don't make mistakes' — entirely useless advice. C++ spent years pretending this worked before accepting you need better tools." — _tialaramex_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48843352)

4. **[Infra & devtools]** [TypeScript 7](https://devblogs.microsoft.com/typescript/announcing-typescript-7-0/) — 703pts · 294c
Why it matters: The Go-native compiler ships as TS 7 — ~10× faster type checking.
HN take: "Jevons paradox — HKTs that made no sense at old speeds are now feasible and will drag the win back down. A smaller slice of a much bigger pie is still more pie." — _dimitropoulos_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48833715)

5. **[Security & policy]** [EU Parliament greenlights Chat Control 1.0](https://www.patrick-breyer.de/en/eu-parliament-greenlights-chat-control-1-0-breyer-our-children-lose-out/) — 1,119pts · 538c
Why it matters: Voluntary CSAM-scanning of private messages extended; mandatory client-side scanning (2.0) still looms.
HN take: "The EU itself admits scanning private messages hasn't improved catching perpetrators. Even the biggest E2EE-breaking lobbyists say criminals move to encrypted platforms — so target those." — _budududuroiu_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48843923)

6. **[Security & policy]** [John Deere right-to-repair under FTC settlement](https://apnews.com/article/john-deere-right-to-repair-agriculture-equipment-cb7514ffedb95c130a976af661f2bc02) — 1,300pts · 274c
Why it matters: FTC forces Deere to unlock Service Advisor diagnostics for owners — first US tractor precedent.
HN take: "$1M fine for probably $10B in profit. I know what lesson I'd learn if my only value was maximizing shareholder value. The compliance part can be dealt with later." — _taurath_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48838876)

7. **[Misc]** [Decoding the obfuscated bash script on a Uniqlo t-shirt](https://tris.sherliker.net/blog/obfuscated-self-evaluating-bash-script-by-cdn-akamai-being-supplied-to-consumers-via-retail-stores/) — 1,458pts · 228c
Why it matters: A Uniqlo shirt carries an obfuscated self-evaluating bash script (Akamai-supplied), reverse-engineered.
HN take: "OCR'ing this is a nightmare and would make a good benchmark for any self-proclaimed OCR/vision model. Wonder if the script itself was LLM-written before obfuscation — lots of hallmark comments." — _Tiberium_
[Discussion](https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48829312)
